engineering.translation: The thief diagnosis. iṣṭān bhogān hi vo devā dāsyante yajña-bhāvitāḥ — the gods, nourished by yajña, will give you desired enjoyments. tair dattān apradāyaibhyo yo bhuṅkte stena eva saḥ — but one who enjoys what is given by them without offering back is verily a thief. Per the Advaita reading, the cosmological-ethical structure makes consumption-without-contribution into theft. Per the Vishishtadvaita reading, the devas have the Supreme Person as their inner Self; not returning offering is misappropriation of what is His. Both readings preserve the sharp word: stena, thief. The engineering analog operates at the operational layer: the engineer who consumes the team's on-call coverage, the team's code-review attention, the team's debugging help — and does not contribute back — is, in the verse's framing, the thief. The metaphysical scope of the original (theft against the divine order) exceeds the operational analog; tag STRETCHED. But the operational claim is real and not soft: consumption without contribution is, structurally, theft.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A senior IC at a startup has been collecting from the team for six months. He has been paged off the rotation when his pages would be inconvenient, has had his GitHub pull requests reviewed by three colleagues simultaneously while he reviews none of theirs, and has used the team's debugging assistance during incidents he was not on call for. His own contributions during this period: zero on-call shifts, four PR reviews (against fifty he received), and zero out-of-hours debugging help to others. When a quarterly retro is run, the team's data is laid out anonymously: PagerDuty pages absorbed, pull requests reviewed, debugging assists given. His name is the outlier in every column on the consumption side and at zero on the contribution side. The principal does not soften this. He says: 'Look at these numbers. By the structure of the rotation, you are taking from the team without giving back. Verse 3.12 calls this stena — thief. I am not using the word lightly. The team has been nourishing you for six months while you have not been nourishing the team. This stops, or your role here ends.' The verse's sharpness is preserved; the operational diagnosis is direct.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader uses 'thief' to describe any temporary asymmetry. Engineers go through phases — onboarding, recovery from burnout, parental leave returns — where consumption legitimately exceeds contribution. The verse's force is structural and sustained, not momentary. A senior who weaponizes 3.12 to shame a junior in their first three months has misread the verse. The diagnosis is for sustained asymmetric extraction, not for temporary imbalance.
engineering.counter_example: When the asymmetry is by mutual agreement — a senior who covers extra on-call this quarter so that a peer can take parental leave, with the explicit understanding that the peer will reciprocate next year — the consumption-contribution accounting is across time, not within a quarter. The verse describes structural extraction, not legitimate sequenced exchange. The diagnostic is whether the loop closes eventually.
engineering.implication: Audit consumption and contribution at the role-pair level, not the individual level. If across two quarters the loop has not closed, the verse's diagnosis applies. The word stena is the original's, and the operational analog preserves it.
engineering.quotable_line: Consumption without contribution is, structurally, theft; the verse's word for it is stena, and the operational analog preserves the sharpness.

iterations.mutation: v1: expanded anvaya (separated stenaḥ/eva/saḥ); added GitHub + PagerDuty explicitly; preserved 'thief' sharpness.
